question: For a 14-year-old girl with primary amenorrhea and stress fracture of the right foot, which evaluation is most appropriate?
options: A. DXA of spine and hip
B. DXA of whole body and spine
C. Prolactin, LH, FSH, estradiol, and 25-hydroxyvitamin D measurements and celiac screen
explanation: The patient is a young girl with primary amenorrhea who has sustained a stress fracture of the right foot. Given her age, the most appropriate initial evaluation would be to assess for underlying causes such as hypogonadism and vitamin D deficiency, which can lead to bone fragility. A lateral spine x-ray (Answer D) is recommended because it can help identify vertebral fractures, which are concerning in a young patient with multiple fractures.
reference: MAIN CONCLUSIONS: The armamentarium to treat pediatric osteoporosis is still limited.
title: When to Worry and What to Do
